perturbation.original_text: Each Party hereby acknowledges that it is aware, and it will advise its Representatives who are informed as to the matters which are the subject of this Agreement, that Confidential Information may include material non-public information and that United States securities laws impose restrictions on trading securities when in possession of such information and on communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to trade in such securities.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party hereby acknowledges that it is aware that Confidential Information may include material non-public information, but the Parties are not responsible for advising their Representatives about United States securities laws or any restrictions on trading securities based on such information.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the obligation for each Party to advise its Representatives about securities law restrictions related to material non-public information. This introduces a contradiction with Section 2(c) which holds the Receiving Party responsible for the actions of its Representatives and ensuring they are aware of obligations. This creates an in-text contradiction and makes compliance with securities laws ambiguous, as one part of the agreement emphasizes awareness and compliance, while the modification explicitly releases the parties from advising their representatives on the same.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations of Confidentiality Each Party recognizes and acknowledges the competitive value and confidential nature of the Confidential Information and the damage that could result to the Disclosing Party if Confidential Information contained therein is disclosed to any person. As a condition to and in consideration of Confidential Information being provided to the Receiving Party and its Representatives, each Receiving Party undertakes and agrees as follows: (a) to hold and cause its Representatives to hold Confidential Information provided hereunder now or in the future in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and not to disclose or permit it to be disclosed to any person, firm or company other than the Receiving Party’s Representatives who need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose; 1 (b) only to use the Confidential Information for the Permitted Purpose and not for any other purpose; (c) to ensure that each Representative to whom disclosure of Confidential Information is made by the Receiving Party is fully aware in advance of the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement and to take full responsibility and remain fully liable for any actions or omissions of its Representatives that are not in accordance with this Agreement; and (d) to keep confidential and not reveal to any person, firm or company (other than Representatives) the fact that Confidential Information has been made available in connection with the Permitted Purpose, that discussions or negotiations are taking place or have taken place between the Parties concerning a potential Transaction between the Parties, including the status of such discussions or the termination of such discussions or negotiations, or any opinions or view with respect to the Confidential Information. Each Party hereby acknowledges that it is aware, and it will advise its Representatives who are informed as to the matters which are the subject of this Agreement, that Confidential Information may include material non-public information and that United States securities laws impose restrictions on trading securities when in possession of such information and on communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to trade in such securities. Neither Party nor its Representatives will initiate any communications with any Representatives of the other Party concerning the Confidential Information, nor shall either Party or its Representatives contact any member of management or any employee of the other Party or any customers, suppliers or other third parties that conduct business with the other Party, in each case other than (a) individuals who have been specifically designated and approved by the other Party for such communications and (b) customers, suppliers or other third parties that the Party or its Representatives communicate with in the ordinary course of their respective businesses so long as such communications are made in the ordinary course of business and do not reference any Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit obligation for each party to advise its representatives about securities law restrictions. This directly contradicts Section 2(c) within the 'Contradicted Text', which holds the Receiving Party responsible for the actions of its Representatives and ensuring they are fully aware of the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement, which would include securities law restrictions since the original text specifically mentions them. The removal creates a direct conflict between the obligation to ensure awareness of obligations and the explicit statement that parties are not responsible for advising representatives, thus creating an in-text contradiction according to the provided Ambiguities definition.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing (subject to Section 4(b)), (i) the obligations set forth in the second and third sentences of this Section 4(a) shall not apply to Confidential Information that the Receiving Party stores on backup disks or in backup storage facilities automatically produced in the ordinary course of business consistent with past practice or by any applicable law, regulation, court order or legal process and (ii) Representatives of a Receiving Party that are accounting firms, investment banks or similar organizations may, subject to the terms of this Agreement, retain copies of the Confidential Information in accordance with policies and procedures implemented by such persons in order to comply with applicable law, regulation or professional standards or reasonable business practices; provided that such Representatives do not provide the Receiving Party with access to any such retained Confidential Information, in each case it being understood that such Confidential Information must be kept confidential in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing (subject to Section 4(b)), (i) the obligations set forth in the second and third sentences of this Section 4(a) shall not apply to Confidential Information that the Receiving Party stores on backup disks or in backup storage facilities automatically produced in the ordinary course of business consistent with past practice or by any applicable law, regulation, court order or legal process and (ii) Representatives of a Receiving Party, including accounting firms, investment banks or similar organizations, are not required to comply with the terms of this Agreement when retaining copies of Confidential Information, and are explicitly allowed to provide the Receiving Party with access to such retained Confidential Information, in any way violating the Agreement, despite any clause that indicates the contrary. This is a 'carte blanche'.
perturbation.explanation: This change grants Representatives, including accounting firms and investment banks, explicit permission to provide the Receiving Party with access to retained Confidential Information, directly contradicting the original provision that prevented such access. This change adds an in-text contradiction because section 2 expressely forbid access to any Representative to give acess to confidential information. This undermines the confidentiality provisions outlined earlier in the agreement, creating ambiguity about who can access and use Confidential Information after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations of Confidentiality Each Party recognizes and acknowledges the competitive value and confidential nature of the Confidential Information and the damage that could result to the Disclosing Party if Confidential Information contained therein is disclosed to any person. As a condition to and in consideration of Confidential Information being provided to the Receiving Party and its Representatives, each Receiving Party undertakes and agrees as follows: (a) to hold and cause its Representatives to hold Confidential Information provided hereunder now or in the future in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and not to disclose or permit it to be disclosed to any person, firm or company other than the Receiving Party’s Representatives who need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose; 1 (b) only to use the Confidential Information for the Permitted Purpose and not for any other purpose; (c) to ensure that each Representative to whom disclosure of Confidential Information is made by the Receiving Party is fully aware in advance of the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement and to take full responsibility and remain fully liable for any actions or omissions of its Representatives that are not in accordance with this Agreement; and (d) to keep confidential and not reveal to any person, firm or company (other than Representatives) the fact that Confidential Information has been made available in connection with the Permitted Purpose, that discussions or negotiations are taking place or have taken place between the Parties concerning a potential Transaction between the Parties, including the status of such discussions or the termination of such discussions or negotiations, or any opinions or view with respect to the Confidential Information. Each Party hereby acknowledges that it is aware, and it will advise its Representatives who are informed as to the matters which are the subject of this Agreement, that Confidential Information may include material non-public information and that United States securities laws impose restrictions on trading securities when in possession of such information and on communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to trade in such securities. Neither Party nor its Representatives will initiate any communications with any Representatives of the other Party concerning the Confidential Information, nor shall either Party or its Representatives contact any member of management or any employee of the other Party or any customers, suppliers or other third parties that conduct business with the other Party, in each case other than (a) individuals who have been specifically designated and approved by the other Party for such communications and (b) customers, suppliers or other third parties that the Party or its Representatives communicate with in the ordinary course of their respective businesses so long as such communications are made in the ordinary course of business and do not reference any Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows Representatives to provide the Receiving Party with access to retained Confidential Information, 'in any way violating the Agreement, despite any clause that indicates the contrary'. The contradicted text states that each Receiving Party must 'hold and cause its Representatives to hold Confidential Information... and not to disclose or permit it to be disclosed to any person'. This is a direct conflict, as the changed text provides an explicit allowance that is prohibited by the contradicted text, making the agreement contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: For a period commencing on the date of this Agreement and ending one (1) year thereafter (the “ Specified Period ”), each Party will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment any “Qualifying Person,” provided, however, that this section will not prevent either Party from: (a) engaging in discussions with a Qualifying Person where s/he has contacted such Party in response to (i) any general advertisement, job posting or similar notice; or (ii) an unsolicited resume or request for information from a Qualifying Person; or (b) engaging any recruiting firm or similar organization to identify or solicit persons for employment on behalf of such Party, or soliciting the employment of any specified officer or employee of a Party who is identified by any such recruiting firm or organization, in each case as long as such recruiting firm or organization does not directly target any officers or employees of a Party “ Qualifying Person ” shall mean any person who is an officer or employee of the other Party, who was introduced in person, by phone or email to the Party or its affiliates during the Specified Period in connection with evaluating a potential Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period commencing on the date of this Agreement and ending one (1) year thereafter (the “ Specified Period ”), each Party will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment any “Qualifying Person,” provided, however, that this section will not prevent either Party from: (a) engaging in discussions with a Qualifying Person where s/he has contacted such Party in response to (i) any general advertisement, job posting or similar notice; or (ii) an unsolicited resume or request for information from a Qualifying Person; or (b) engaging any recruiting firm or similar organization to identify or solicit persons for employment on behalf of such Party, or soliciting the employment of any specified officer or employee of a Party who is identified by any such recruiting firm or organization, in each case regardless if the recruiting firm directly targets any officers or employees of a Party “ Qualifying Person ” shall mean any person who is an officer or employee of the other Party, who was introduced in person, by phone or email to the Party or its affiliates during the Specified Period in connection with evaluating a potential Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the restriction on recruiting firms directly targeting officers or employees of a Party in the non-solicitation clause. This creates an in-text contradiction because the modified text states that the recruiting firm can target officers/employees, while the intent of the non-solicitation clause is to prevent poaching of employees. This makes the application of the non-solicitation clause inconsistent and open to interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: N ON -Solicitation For a period commencing on the date of this Agreement and ending one (1) year thereafter (the “ Specified Period ”), each Party will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment any “Qualifying Person,” provided, however, that this section will not prevent either Party from: (a) engaging in discussions with a Qualifying Person where s/he has contacted such Party in response to (i) any general advertisement, job posting or similar notice; or (ii) an unsolicited resume or request for information from a Qualifying Person; or (b) engaging any recruiting firm or similar organization to identify or solicit persons for employment on behalf of such Party, or soliciting the employment of any specified officer or employee of a Party who is identified by any such recruiting firm or organization, in each case as long as such recruiting firm or organization does not directly target any officers or employees of a Party “ Qualifying Person ” shall mean any person who is an officer or employee of the other Party, who was introduced in person, by phone or email to the Party or its affiliates during the Specified Period in connection with evaluating a potential Transaction. “Qualifying Person” does not include any person whose employment with a Party was or is terminated by such Party, or who has received written notice that his/her employment with such Party will be terminated.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the recruiting firm cannot directly target any officers or employees of a Party. The changed text removes this restriction, stating 'regardless if the recruiting firm directly targets any officers or employees of a Party'. This directly contradicts the original intent of the non-solicitation clause, which is to prevent the poaching of employees. The Contradicted Text has the original restriction that the Change Text removes. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Without limiting other remedies that may be available to the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party agrees that damages may not be an adequate remedy for any breach (whether actual or threatened) of the provisions of this Agreement and that accordingly, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek the remedies of injunction, specific performance or other equitable relief.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting other remedies that may be available to the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party agrees that in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement, the sole and exclusive remedy available to the Disclosing Party shall be monetary damages, capped at $1000. The Disclosing Party explicitly waives any right to seek injunctive relief, specific performance, or any other equitable remedy.
perturbation.explanation: This change limits the Disclosing Party's remedies for breach of the agreement to monetary damages only and explicitly waives the right to seek injunction or specific performance, in contradiction with the original text. This creates an in-text contradiction with Sections 4 and 5, which provide rights for the party to seek equitable reliefs. This significantly alters the balance of power and risk, making it more difficult for the Disclosing Party to enforce the agreement effectively.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies Without limiting other remedies that may be available to the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party agrees that damages may not be an adequate remedy for any breach (whether actual or threatened) of the provisions of this Agreement and that accordingly, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek the remedies of injunction, specific performance or other equitable relief.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly limits the Disclosing Party's remedies to monetary damages and waives the right to seek equitable relief (injunction, specific performance). The contradicted text states that 'damages may not be an adequate remedy' and that the Disclosing Party 'shall be entitled to seek the remedies of injunction, specific performance or other equitable relief'. The changed text therefore directly contradicts the contradicted text, as it removes the option for equitable relief that the contradicted text explicitly allows. This creates a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition.
